source_pr.title: plan: fix a bug when applying `top-n push down` rule.
source_pr.body: If the limit operator is combined with a sort operator, it's a new operator and should not be eliminated.

symptoms: sql: "select * from (select * from t use index() order by b) t left join t t1 on t.a=t1.a limit 10", (plan/physical_plan_test.go)
failure_signature: sql: "select * from (select * from t use index() order by b) t left join t t1 on t.a=t1.a limit 10", (plan/physical_plan_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it relied on nondeterministic result ordering (e.g., missing ORDER BY / unsorted assertions). Result ordering is not guaranteed unless explicitly enforced. This PR stabilizes it by: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.
fix_pattern: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.

evidence: evidence: sql: "select * from (select * from t use index() order by b) t left join t t1 on t.a=t1.a limit 10", (plan/physical_plan_test.go)
